followupAnswerBank.question: How do you pass parameters to a CDS view in ABAP?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ABAP Open SQL mein, parameters CDS view ko FROM clause mein view name ke baad parentheses mein append karke pass hote hain. Har parameter parameter_name = @abap_variable ya parameter_name = 'literal_value' ki form mein specify hota hai. Saare parameters provide karne hote hain — CDS parameters mein koi default values nahi hain. Agar koi parameter omit ho jaye, ABAP syntax error ya runtime error aata hai. ABAP compiler parameter names aur types compile time pe validate karta hai agar CDS view properly activated aur referenced hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In ABAP Open SQL, parameters are passed to a CDS view by appending them in parentheses after the view name in the FROM clause. Each parameter is specified as parameter_name = @abap_variable or parameter_name = 'literal_value'. All parameters must be provided — there are no default values in CDS parameters. If a parameter is omitted, an ABAP syntax error or runtime error occurs. The ABAP compiler validates parameter names and types at compile time if the CDS view is properly activated and referenced.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Common mistake in team: Developer forgot one parameter in ABAP call — compiler did not catch it (older ABAP version without strict syntax check). Runtime exception: CX_SY_DYNAMIC_OSQL_SEMANTICS — parameter missing. Fix: add missing parameter. Best practice adopted: always use Eclipse ADT content assist (Ctrl+Space) when writing SELECT FROM parameterized CDS view — ADT auto-suggests all parameters.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Parameter name typo karna — ABAP runtime exception at SELECT statement. Ya parameter value type mismatch — abap.dats parameter mein numc value pass karna. Eclipse ADT syntax check aur content assist use karo to avoid these errors.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
* Correct: all parameters provided with @ prefix
DATA: lv_date TYPE d,
      lv_bukrs TYPE bukrs.
lv_date  = sy-datum.
lv_bukrs = '1000'.

SELECT DocumentNumber, PostingDate
  FROM zcds_fi_docs_param(
         p_budat = @lv_date,
         p_bukrs = @lv_bukrs )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs).

* Also valid: literal values
SELECT DocumentNumber
  FROM zcds_fi_docs_param(
         p_budat = '20240101',
         p_bukrs = '1000' )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs2).

followupAnswerBank.question: Can you use CDS input parameters in associations ON condition?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, CDS input parameters association ON conditions mein $parameters.param_name ke through reference ho sakte hain, jaise woh main WHERE clause mein use ho sakte hain. Ye caller-provided values ke basis pe dynamic association resolution enable karta hai — for example, ek language parameter jo control kare ki kaunsi text association resolve ho, ya ek currency code parameter jo exchange rate table associations mein use ho. Parameter value puri view definition mein propagate hoti hai including saari association ON conditions jo isko reference karti hain.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, CDS input parameters can be referenced using $parameters.param_name in association ON conditions, just as they can be used in the main WHERE clause. This enables dynamic association resolution based on caller-provided values — for example, a language parameter controlling which text association resolves, or a currency code parameter used in exchange rate table associations. The parameter value propagates through the entire view definition including all association ON conditions referencing it.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Multi-currency reporting view: ZCDS_MATERIAL_PRICE had parameter p_waers (currency). Association to KONP (price condition records) had ON condition with $parameters.p_waers for WAERS field filter. Each caller passes their target currency — only price records for that currency are fetched via the association. Without parameter in association ON condition — all currency price records would need a further filter, or a separate view per currency would be needed.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Association ON condition mein hardcoded value likhna jahan caller-specific value chahiye — jaise hardcoded currency 'USD' when different callers need different currencies. $parameters use karo for dynamic association behavior.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
define view ZCDS_PRICE_PARAM
  with parameters
    p_waers : waers,
    p_date  : abap.dats
  as select from mara
  " Association ON uses $parameters
  association [0..*] to a004 as _PriceCondition
    on  $projection.matnr    = _PriceCondition.matnr
    and _PriceCondition.waers = $parameters.p_waers
    and _PriceCondition.datbi >= $parameters.p_date
    and _PriceCondition.datab <= $parameters.p_date
{
  key mara.matnr,
      mara.mtart,
      _PriceCondition
}

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the difference between CDS parameters and CDS session variables?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS input parameters caller dwara consumption time pe explicitly provide kiye jaate hain — caller ko saare declared parameters ke liye values pass karne hote hain. CDS session variables SAP runtime dwara current user ke session context ke basis pe automatically provide hoti hain — koi explicit passing nahi chahiye. Key session variables: $session.system_language logon language ke liye, $session.client SAP client ke liye, $session.user logged-in username ke liye. Parameters use karo jab filter value har call mein vary kare aur caller dwara control hoti ho. Session variables use karo jab filter current user ke session context pe based ho.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS input parameters are explicitly provided by the caller at consumption time — the caller must pass values for all declared parameters. CDS session variables are automatically provided by the SAP runtime based on the current user's session context — no explicit passing needed. Key session variables: $session.system_language for logon language, $session.client for the SAP client, $session.user for the logged-in username. Use parameters when the filter value varies per call and must be controlled by the caller. Use session variables when the filter is based on the current user's session context.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Combined usage in project: Financial report CDS view — parameter p_gjahr (fiscal year, caller provides) AND session variable $session.user for audit trail (automatic). Language texts filtered by $session.system_language (automatic — no parameter needed). Authorization check via DCL references user session automatically. Three different 'variable' sources in one view: explicit parameter, session variable, DCL session context.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: $session variables ko manually pass karne ki koshish karna parameter ki tarah — ye nahi hota. $session variables system-provided hain, caller control nahi kar sakta. $session.system_language parameter ki tarah treat mat karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
define view ZCDS_COMBINED_VARS
  with parameters
    p_gjahr : gjahr     " explicit - caller provides
  as select from bkpf
  left outer join t001
    on bkpf.bukrs = t001.bukrs
  left outer join t001t
    on t001.bukrs   = t001t.bukrs
    and t001t.spras = $session.system_language  " session var
{
  key bkpf.belnr,
      bkpf.bukrs,
      t001t.butxt as CompanyName,    " in user language
      bkpf.budat
}
where bkpf.gjahr = $parameters.p_gjahr  " parameter
  and bkpf.mandt = $session.client

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you use parameterized CDS view in Fiori/OData?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab parameterized CDS view OData ke through expose hoti hai — chahe @OData.publish: true annotation se ya SEGW se — view parameters metadata mein OData entity key ka part ban jaate hain. OData callers parameter values URL mein entity key ke part ke roop mein provide karte hain. Fiori applications ke liye, UI5 binding parameter passing automatically handle karta hai jab SmartFilterBar ya manual binding values bhejta hai. Practice mein, parameterized CDS views analytical Fiori tiles ke liye commonly use hoti hain jahan filter context (date, company code) tile configuration se pass hota hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a parameterized CDS view is exposed via OData — either with @OData.publish: true annotation or via SEGW — the view parameters become part of the OData entity key in the metadata. OData callers provide parameter values as part of the entity key in the URL. For Fiori applications, the UI5 binding handles the parameter passing automatically when the SmartFilterBar or manual binding sends values. In practice, parameterized CDS views are commonly used for analytical Fiori tiles where the filter context (date, company code) is passed from the tile configuration.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Fiori tile configuration: Monthly AR aging report. CDS view with parameters p_bukrs and p_keydate. OData service exposed. Tile configured with static parameter values — company code from user's personalization, key date as today ($today). User clicks tile — tile configuration passes parameters to OData call — CDS view filters at HANA level. Different users see different company code data from same CDS view based on tile configuration. One CDS view, multiple tile configurations for different company codes.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: @OData.publish: true lagana parameterized view pe aur phir plain EntitySet access karna without parameters — OData error: key not complete. Parameterized view ke liye OData call mein hamesha parameters provide karna mandatory hai.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
@OData.publish: true
@EndUserText.label: 'AR Aging Report'
define view ZCDS_AR_AGING
  with parameters
    p_bukrs  : bukrs,
    p_keydate: abap.dats
  as select from bsid
{
  key bsid.belnr  as Document,
      bsid.kunnr  as Customer,
      bsid.dmbtr  as Amount,
      bsid.faedt  as DueDate
}
where bsid.bukrs = $parameters.p_bukrs
  and bsid.faedt <= $parameters.p_keydate

" OData URL:
" /ArAgingSet(p_bukrs='1000',p_keydate='20240101')

followupAnswerBank.question: Can parameterized CDS views be used in ABAP joins?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Haan, parameterized CDS views ABAP Open SQL JOIN operations mein use ho sakti hain — parameters parentheses mein FROM clause ke part ke roop mein provide karo. Tum parameterized CDS view ko regular database tables ya other CDS views ke saath join kar sakte ho. Parameter values FROM clause mein JOIN ke point pe pass hoti hain. Ye parameterized CDS logic ko additional ABAP-level joins ke saath combine karne deta hai jab CDS view mein already saare required data nahi hote.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Yes, parameterized CDS views can be used in ABAP Open SQL JOIN operations — provide the parameters in parentheses as part of the FROM clause. You can join a parameterized CDS view with regular database tables or other CDS views. The parameter values are passed at the point of the JOIN in the FROM clause. This allows combining parameterized CDS logic with additional ABAP-level joins when the CDS view does not already contain all required data.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Report requirement: List of open invoices (from parameterized CDS view filtering by date and company) joined with customer credit limit (from KNB1). CDS view already had parameter for date and bukrs. ABAP: `FROM zcds_open_invoices( p_date = @date, p_bukrs = @bukrs ) AS inv INNER JOIN knb1 AS cr ON inv.kunnr = cr.kunnr`. Clean combination: CDS handles date/company filter pushdown, ABAP handles the credit table join.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Parameterized CDS view ko FROM clause mein use karna aur parameters bhoolna — syntax error ya runtime error. Parameters ka banana zaruri hai, koi default nahi hota.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
* Parameterized CDS view in ABAP JOIN
DATA: lv_date  TYPE d VALUE '20240101',
      lv_bukrs TYPE bukrs VALUE '1000'.

SELECT inv~Document,
       inv~Customer,
       inv~Amount,
       cr~klimk AS CreditLimit
  FROM zcds_ar_aging( p_bukrs   = @lv_bukrs,
                      p_keydate = @lv_date ) AS inv
  INNER JOIN knb1 AS cr
    ON  inv~Customer = cr~kunnr
    AND cr~bukrs     = @lv_bukrs
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_result)
  ORDER BY inv~Amount DESCENDING.

followupAnswerBank.question: What are the limitations of CDS input parameters?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: CDS input parameters ki kuch limitations hain: Pehla, koi default values nahi — har caller ko saare declared parameters provide karne hote hain. Doosra, sirf scalar values supported hain — tum internal tables, ranges, ya SELECT-OPTIONS parameters ke roop mein pass nahi kar sakte. Teesra, parameters dynamic sorting ya dynamic field selection ke liye use nahi ho sakte — sirf filtering ke liye. Chautha, agar tumhe range filtering (BETWEEN, IN list) chahiye, parameters sirf single values accept karte hain, isliye range logic view selection ke baad ABAP WHERE mein handle karna padta hai. Paancha, parameterized views kuch DDL contexts mein use nahi ho sakti jaise CDS view extension.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: CDS input parameters have several limitations: First, no default values — every caller must provide all declared parameters. Second, only scalar values are supported — you cannot pass internal tables, ranges, or SELECT-OPTIONS as parameters. Third, parameters cannot be used for dynamic sorting or dynamic field selection — only for filtering. Fourth, if you need range filtering (BETWEEN, IN list), parameters only accept single values, so range logic must be handled in ABAP WHERE after the view selection. Fifth, parameterized views cannot be used in some DDL contexts like CDS view extension.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Design limitation hit in project: Report needed date range filter — from date and to date. CDS parameter approach: two parameters p_datfrom and p_datoto, WHERE budat BETWEEN $parameters.p_datfrom AND $parameters.p_datoto. Works for date range. But when user needed 'multiple specific dates' not a range — CDS parameters cannot handle it. Solution: ABAP level post-filter for the multi-date scenario, CDS parameter for the common range case.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: SELECT-OPTIONS ya ranges directly CDS parameter mein pass karne ki koshish — nahi hota. CDS parameters sirf single scalar value accept karte hain. Range filtering ke liye two parameters (from/to) use karo ya ABAP mein additional WHERE lagao.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Limitation 1: No default values
" Both parameters mandatory - no optional params
define view ZCDS_STRICT_PARAMS
  with parameters
    p_from : abap.dats,   " must provide
    p_to   : abap.dats    " must provide
  as select from bkpf
{ key bkpf.belnr, bkpf.budat }
where bkpf.budat between $parameters.p_from
                      and $parameters.p_to

" Limitation 2: Cannot pass ranges - workaround
DATA: lt_range TYPE RANGE OF d.
APPEND VALUE #( sign = 'I' option = 'EQ'
                low  = '20240101' ) TO lt_range.
" Must use ABAP WHERE for range - no CDS param for this
SELECT belnr FROM zcds_fi_docs_param(
                     p_budat = @sy-datum,
                     p_bukrs = '1000' )
  WHERE budat IN @lt_range    " ABAP level range
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs).

followupAnswerBank.question: How do CDS parameters differ from ABAP selection screen parameters?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: ABAP selection screen parameters user-facing input fields hain — users SAP GUI screen ke through values enter karte hain. Ye ranges, multiple values, complex SELECT-OPTIONS, aur validation events (AT SELECTION-SCREEN) support karte hain. CDS input parameters developer-facing hain — ye code level pe FROM clause mein programmatically pass hote hain, bina kisi UI ke. Ye sirf scalar values support karte hain aur koi events nahi hote. Reports mein, dono saath use hote hain: selection screen user input capture karta hai, phir ABAP program woh values CDS parameters ki tarah SELECT statement mein pass karta hai, user-friendly input ko database-level filtering ke saath combine karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: ABAP selection screen parameters are user-facing input fields — users enter values through an SAP GUI screen. They support ranges, multiple values, complex SELECT-OPTIONS, and validation events (AT SELECTION-SCREEN). CDS input parameters are developer-facing — they are passed programmatically in FROM clause at code level, with no UI. They support only scalar values and have no events. In reports, both are used together: the selection screen captures user input, then the ABAP program passes those values as CDS parameters in the SELECT statement, combining user-friendly input with database-level filtering.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Classic report modernization: Old ALV report had PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs, p_date TYPE d on selection screen. New version: kept selection screen for user input, replaced internal table processing with CDS parameterized view. `SELECT FROM zcds_fi_docs_param( p_budat = @p_date, p_bukrs = @p_bukrs )`. Best of both worlds: familiar selection screen for users, HANA pushdown for performance.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: CDS parameter ko selection screen alternative samajhna — ye nahi hain. CDS parameter koi UI nahi provide karta. Selection screen user interaction ke liye zaruri hai. CDS parameter database efficiency ke liye. Dono complementary hain, alternative nahi.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
REPORT z_report_with_cds_param.

" Selection screen - user input UI
SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK b1.
  PARAMETERS: p_bukrs TYPE bukrs OBLIGATORY,
              p_date  TYPE d DEFAULT sy-datum.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK b1.

START-OF-SELECTION.
  " Pass selection screen values as CDS parameters
  SELECT DocumentNumber, PostingDate, Currency
    FROM zcds_fi_docs_param(
           p_budat = @p_date,    " from sel-screen
           p_bukrs = @p_bukrs )  " from sel-screen
    INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs).

  " Display in ALV
  cl_salv_table=>factory( IMPORTING r_salv_table = DATA(lo_alv)
                           CHANGING  t_table = lt_docs ).
  lo_alv->display( ).

followupAnswerBank.question: What happens when you call a parameterized CDS view without providing all parameters?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Agar parameterized CDS view ko bina saare required parameters provide kiye call kiya jaye, result ABAP version aur syntax mode pe depend karta hai. Strict ABAP syntax mode (ABAP 7.54 se available) mein, missing parameter compile time pe Eclipse ADT mein catch hota hai — program run hone se pehle hi syntax error. Older syntax modes mein, error runtime pe CX_SY_DYNAMIC_OSQL_SEMANTICS exception ke roop mein surface karta hai jab SELECT execute hoti hai. CDS view partially execute nahi hoti — agar koi parameter missing hai toh entirely fail hoti hai. Best practice: strict syntax checking ke saath Eclipse ADT use karo parameter issues development time pe catch karne ke liye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: If a parameterized CDS view is called without providing all required parameters, the result depends on ABAP version and syntax mode. In strict ABAP syntax mode (available from ABAP 7.54), the missing parameter is caught at compile time in Eclipse ADT — syntax error before the program even runs. In older syntax modes, the error surfaces at runtime as a CX_SY_DYNAMIC_OSQL_SEMANTICS exception when the SELECT executes. The CDS view does not execute partially — it fails entirely if any parameter is missing. Best practice: use Eclipse ADT with strict syntax checking to catch parameter issues at development time.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Production incident: ABAP program called parameterized CDS view, worked in development (all test cases had both parameters set). In production, a code path where p_bukrs was initial (empty) slipped through. Runtime exception in production — report crashed. Fix: add ABAP check before SELECT — if parameter variable is initial, raise meaningful error or skip CDS call. Lesson: validate parameter values before passing to CDS view.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Assume karna ki initial/empty ABAP variable CDS parameter ke liye valid hai. CDS view sirf value validate nahi karta — database ko bhejta hai. Empty bukrs '' sab records match kar sakta hai depending on table data. Always validate parameter values in ABAP before SELECT.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
* Safe parameterized CDS view call pattern
DATA: lv_bukrs TYPE bukrs,
      lv_date  TYPE d.

" Get values (from selection screen, user defaults, etc.)
lv_bukrs = p_bukrs.
lv_date  = p_date.

" Validate before calling CDS view
IF lv_bukrs IS INITIAL OR lv_date IS INITIAL.
  MESSAGE 'Company code and date are required' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.

" Safe to call - parameters validated
SELECT DocumentNumber, PostingDate
  FROM zcds_fi_docs_param(
         p_budat = @lv_date,
         p_bukrs = @lv_bukrs )
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_docs).

IF lt_docs IS INITIAL.
  MESSAGE 'No documents found for given criteria' TYPE 'I'.
ENDIF.

cds-views-007.easyUnderstanding: CDS Access Control aur DCL — ye CDS ka security guard hai. Sochte ho — tumne ek CDS view banaya jo sab employees ki salary dikhata hai. Ab kya tum chahte ho ki har ABAP developer ya user ye data dekh sake? Nahi. DCL (Data Control Language) CDS view pe ek automatic security layer lagata hai. Tum define karte ho: 'Sirf wo log ye data dekh sakte hain jinke paas S_CARRID authorization object mein ACTVT 03 hai aur unka CARRID match karta ho.' Ye rules DEFINE ROLE mein likhte hain — ek alag CDS DCL object. CDS view ko @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK se annotate karte hain — ab connection ban gayi. Magical part: ab jo bhi ye view access kare — ABAP SELECT, OData call, Fiori app, SE16N — authorization check automatic hota hai. Koi extra AUTHORITY-CHECK command nahi likhna padta. Data automatically filter hota hai based on user's authorizations. Ek aur important point: SE16N directly table access karta hai — DCL bypass ho sakta hai SE16N mein. Ye interview mein puchha jaata hai. ABAP SELECT FROM cds_view pe DCL kaam karta hai — direct table SELECT pe nahi.
cds-views-007.hinglishMasterExplanation:
CDS DCL — complete interview guide:

**Three-layer setup:**
1. CDS view mein annotation: `@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK`
2. DCL source object create karo (same name as CDS view)
3. DCL defines: WHO can access WHICH data

**Authorization check options:**
- `#CHECK` — DCL attached hai, check karo (recommended for production)
- `#NOT_REQUIRED` — no authorization check (development/test only)
- `#PRIVILEGED_ONLY` — only privileged users (RAP specific)
- `#INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT` — inherit from parent view

**DCL syntax:**
```
@MappingRole: true
define role ZROLE_NAME {
  grant select on ZCDS_VIEW_NAME
    where (field1, field2)
           = aspect pfcg_auth(AUTH_OBJECT, FIELD1, ACTVT = '03');
}
```

**pfcg_auth aspect:**
- Authorization object reference
- Maps CDS view fields to authorization object fields
- ACTVT activity code (03 = display, 02 = change, 01 = create)

**How it works at runtime:**
```
User runs SELECT FROM zcds_view
    ↓
ABAP adds DCL WHERE conditions automatically:
    WHERE carrid IN (user's authorized CARRIDs)
    ↓
HANA executes with authorization filter — transparent to ABAP code
```

**SE16N bypass — key interview point:**
SE16N accesses database table directly — not CDS view. DCL protects CDS view access. SE16N shows raw table data without DCL. Solution: Use `SELECT without AUTHORITY-CHECK` pattern or restrict SE16N access via profile. Never rely on DCL alone for SE16N data protection.

**DCL object naming:**
Same name as CDS view — ZCDS_VIEW and ZCDS_VIEW (DCL) — system automatically links them when name matches.
cds-views-007.interviewMeKyaBolnaHai:
Interview Me Bolna Hai:
'DCL — Data Control Language — in CDS is used to define row-level access control on CDS views. You annotate the CDS view with @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK, then create a separate DCL source object with the same name as the view. The DCL defines which users can select which rows using authorization objects referenced via the pfcg_auth aspect. At runtime, when any consumer accesses the CDS view — whether an ABAP program, OData call, or Fiori app — the DCL conditions are automatically appended to the WHERE clause. The ABAP code does not need explicit AUTHORITY-CHECK statements — authorization is centralized in the DCL layer.'

Hinglish tip: Interview mein DCL ka sabse impactful point ye hai — 'DCL replaces manual authority-check in every ABAP program — centralized authorization.' Phir SE16N bypass zaroor mention karo — 'SE16N directly table access karta hai, DCL bypass hota hai SE16N mein — isiliye SE16N access bhi restrict karna chahiye sensitive data ke liye.' Ye shows ki tum security implications samajhte ho — interviewer impress hoga.
cds-views-007.interviewScriptHinglish: DCL ke through CDS access control CDS views pe row-level security provide karta hai, jo sab consumers ke liye transparent hota hai. View ko @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK se annotate karte hain, aur ek DCL source file DEFINE ROLE rules declare karti hai. pfcg_auth aspect DCL conditions ko PFCG authorization objects pe map karta hai taaki user-specific filters har SELECT mein add ho jaayein — chahe ABAP Open SQL se ho, OData se ho, ya Fiori se. Inheritance DCL rules ko dependent CDS views ke through propagate karne deta hai. SE16N DCL bypass karta hai kyunki yeh tables ko directly read karta hai. Mere project mein sales organization pe ek DCL ne automatically sab consumers ke across per-user data restrict kiya. Business value centralised, consistent security hai jo normal SAP consumption se bypass karna impossible hai.
cds-views-007.interviewScriptEnglish: CDS access control via DCL provides row-level security on CDS views, transparent to all consumers. The view is annotated with @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK, and a DCL source file declares DEFINE ROLE rules. The pfcg_auth aspect maps DCL conditions to PFCG authorization objects so user-specific filters are added to every SELECT — whether from ABAP Open SQL, OData, or Fiori. Inheritance allows DCL rules to propagate through dependent CDS views. SE16N bypasses DCL because it reads tables directly. In our project a DCL on sales organization automatically restricted data per user across all consumers. The business value is centralised, consistent security that is impossible to bypass through normal SAP consumption.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Annotation enabling DCL enforcement on the CDS view
DCL keyword introducing an access role for a CDS entity
DCL aspect that links access rules to PFCG authorization objects
DCL clause specifying read access policy on the target view
DCL rules propagate through dependent CDS views automatically
Table browser that bypasses DCL because it queries the table directly

commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Opens the view to every user, creating an audit-grade security gap
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Keep #CHECK by default; only use #NOT_REQUIRED with documented business reason

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Forgetting to transport the DCL file with the CDS view
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Target system enforces no rules and exposes data
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Include both DDLS and DCLS objects in the same transport request

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Relying on SE16N to test DCL behaviour
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: SE16N bypasses DCL entirely; tests look pass even with broken rules
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Test via ADT data preview, OData service, or ABAP SELECT to confirm DCL filtering

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Defining overlapping rules that allow more than intended
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: DCL conditions are OR-ed, so loose rules grant unwanted access
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Review each rule, prefer specific conditions, and test with multiple user profiles

cds-views-007.quickRevisionNotes:
What: Row-level security model on CDS views using DCL access roles.

Used for:
- Centralised authorization for Fiori, OData, and ABAP consumers
- PFCG-based row filtering through pfcg_auth aspect
- Audit-grade data protection in S/4HANA reporting

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
- DEFINE ROLE name_role { GRANT SELECT ON ... WHERE ... ; }
- pfcg_auth(AUTH_OBJ, FIELD, ACTVT='03')
- Transport both DDLS (view) and DCLS (DCL) together

Interview keyword: centralized row-level security via DCL

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the @AccessControl.authorizationCheck annotation and its values?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: @AccessControl.authorizationCheck annotation control karta hai ki CDS view access hone pe authorization kaise enforce hogi. #CHECK matlab same name wala DCL source hona chahiye aur uski conditions runtime pe automatically apply hoti hain — production views ke liye recommended value. #NOT_REQUIRED saari authorization checking disable kar deta hai — sirf development mein ya technical utility views ke liye acceptable jinme koi sensitive data nahi hai. #PRIVILEGED_ONLY access ko sirf internal RAP framework calls tak restrict karta hai, direct external access ko block karta hai — RAP draft tables aur internal views ke liye use hota hai. Hamesha is annotation ko explicitly specify karo — system defaults pe rely karna unpredictable behavior create karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The @AccessControl.authorizationCheck annotation controls how authorization is enforced when a CDS view is accessed. #CHECK means a DCL source with the same name must exist and its conditions are automatically applied at runtime — this is the recommended value for production views. #NOT_REQUIRED disables all authorization checking — acceptable only in development or for technical utility views with no sensitive data. #PRIVILEGED_ONLY restricts access to internal RAP framework calls only, blocking direct external access — used for RAP draft tables and internal views. Always explicitly specify this annotation — relying on system defaults creates unpredictable behavior.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security audit finding in project: 12 CDS views had @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED in production — set during development, never changed. External audit flagged this. Remediation: create DCL objects for all sensitive views, change annotation to #CHECK. Lesson: development annotation must be removed before transport to production. Now CI/CD pipeline includes a check — transport blocked if #NOT_REQUIRED found in productive system.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: #NOT_REQUIRED production views mein transport karna — common mistake during initial development. Aur: annotation bilkul nahi lagana — system default apply hota hai jo environment pe depend karta hai. Always explicitly set the annotation and review before transport.

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you create and activate a DCL source in Eclipse ADT?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Eclipse ADT mein: File menu → New → Other → Core Data Services folder → Access Control. DCL object name enter karo — ye CDS view name se exactly match karna chahiye automatic linking ke liye. DCL editor template provide karta hai. CDS view name pe grant select ke saath role define karo, where clause add karo pfcg_auth aspect use karte hue authorization object, field mappings, aur activity code ke saath. Ctrl+F3 ya right-click Activate se activate karo. Test karo CDS view se SELECT ABAP mein run karke — sirf user ke authorization profile se matching data return hona chahiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: In Eclipse ADT: File menu → New → Other → Core Data Services folder → Access Control. Enter the DCL object name — it must match the CDS view name exactly for automatic linking. The DCL editor provides a template. Define the role with grant select on the CDS view name, add the where clause using pfcg_auth aspect with the authorization object, field mappings, and activity code. Activate using Ctrl+F3 or right-click Activate. Test by running a SELECT from the CDS view in ABAP — only data matching the user's authorization profile should be returned.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: DCL creation for HR data: ZCDS_EMPLOYEE_DATA CDS view showing employee personal data. DCL needed: only HR managers should see employees in their own organizational unit. Authorization object P_ORGIN, field ORGEH (org unit). DCL: `where (OrgUnit) = aspect pfcg_auth(P_ORGIN, ORGEH, ACTVT = '03')`. After activation: SELECT returned only employees in logged-in manager's org unit automatically. Tested with 3 different user accounts — each saw only their unit's employees.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: DCL object ka naam CDS view se alag rakhna — linking nahi hota. @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK lagaya hai lekin DCL nahi bana — runtime error: access control object not found. DCL naam bilkul same hona chahiye CDS view se.

followupAnswerBank.question: How does DCL work with OData and Fiori applications?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab DCL wali CDS view OData ke through expose hoti hai — chahe @OData.publish: true se ya SEGW se — DCL authorization OData requests ke liye transparently enforce hoti hai. OData framework logged-in user ke context mein run hota hai, aur jab woh underlying CDS view query karta hai, DCL WHERE conditions automatically apply hoti hain. Fiori application developer ko koi authorization filtering implement karne ki zarurat nahi — DCL centrally handle karta hai. Iska matlab same CDS view safely different user groups ko expose ho sakti hai, har group apna authorized data subset hi dekhta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a CDS view with DCL is exposed via OData — either through @OData.publish: true or SEGW — the DCL authorization is transparently enforced for OData requests. The OData framework runs under the logged-in user's context, and when it queries the underlying CDS view, the DCL WHERE conditions are automatically applied. The Fiori application developer does not need to implement any authorization filtering — the DCL handles it centrally. This means the same CDS view can be safely exposed to different user groups, with each group seeing only their authorized data subset.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Sales data Fiori app security: CDS view ZCDS_SALES_DATA exposed via OData. DCL restricted by sales organization (VKORG) using authorization object V_VBAK_VKO. Sales manager Germany: OData response shows only German VKORG orders. Sales manager USA: same OData URL, same Fiori app, but response shows only US VKORG orders. DCL works transparently — one app, one CDS view, one OData service, but automatic data isolation per user's authorization.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sochna ki OData security alag se implement karni padti hai jab DCL hai. DCL CDS level pe kaam karta hai — OData consumers ko automatically protection milti hai. Extra OData-level filtering unnecessary hai agar DCL properly defined hai.

followupAnswerBank.question: Can you combine multiple authorization conditions in one DCL?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DCL multiple authorization conditions support karta hai AND aur OR operators se combine karke. Tum require kar sakte ho ki user ko multiple fields ke liye simultaneously authorized hona chahiye — for example, sales organization aur distribution channel dono. Ya tum access allow kar sakte ho agar multiple conditions mein se koi ek meet ho — different user types ke liye OR logic. Conditions same DCL mein different authorization objects reference kar sakti hain. Ye complex, real-world authorization scenarios enable karta hai jaise: 'User ke paas VKORG auth AND VTWEG auth honi chahiye' ya 'User ke paas CARRID auth ho OR woh S_TCODE full access ke saath superuser ho'.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: DCL supports multiple authorization conditions combined with AND and OR operators. You can require that a user must be authorized for multiple fields simultaneously — for example, both sales organization and distribution channel. Or you can allow access if any one of multiple conditions is met — OR logic for different user types. Conditions can reference different authorization objects in the same DCL. This enables complex, real-world authorization scenarios like: 'User must have VKORG auth AND VTWEG auth' or 'User has CARRID auth OR is a superuser with S_TCODE full access'.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Complex DCL requirement: Airline booking CDS view. Rule 1: Regular agents see only their assigned carrier (S_CARRID, CARRID). Rule 2: Supervisor role (identified by special S_DEVELOP auth object) can see all carriers. DCL combined: `where (CarrierID) = aspect pfcg_auth(S_CARRID, CARRID, ACTVT = '03') OR aspect pfcg_auth(S_DEVELOP, DEVCLASS = '$')`. Supervisors with S_DEVELOP full access see all data. Agents see only their carriers. One DCL handles both user types.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Bahut complex DCL likhna jo maintain karna mushkil ho. Prefer simple DCL with clear authorization logic. Complex scenarios ke liye — multiple DCL roles combine karo (DCL supports role combination) rather than one mega-DCL with complex OR/AND chains.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Multiple conditions in DCL
@MappingRole: true
define role ZCDS_BOOKING_ACCESS {
  grant select on ZCDS_AIRLINE_BOOKING
    " Condition 1: standard agents - carrier restricted
    where (CarrierID, ConnID)
           = aspect pfcg_auth( S_CARRID,
                               CARRID,
                               CONNID,
                               ACTVT = '03' )
    " OR Condition 2: admin users - all access
    or  (BookingClass)
           = aspect pfcg_auth( S_DEVELOP,
                               DEVCLASS,
                               ACTVT = '03' );
}

followupAnswerBank.question: Why does SE16N bypass DCL and what is the alternative?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: SAP GUI mein SE16N database tables ko directly access karta hai — ye physical table pe direct SELECT construct karta hai, CDS layer aur kisi bhi associated DCL ko completely bypass karte hue. DCL sirf tab activate hota hai jab CDS view access hoti hai — jab underlying table directly query hoti hai toh nahi. SE16N bypass se sensitive data protect karne ke liye: sensitive tables ke liye S_TABU_DIS authorization object aur table groups ke through SE16N access restrict karo. Alternatively, users ko educate karo ki data SE16N ki jagah CDS-view-based Fiori apps ke through access karen. Newer SAP systems mein, tum SE16N mein CDS view name directly access kar sakte ho — us case mein DCL apply hota hai. Par SE16N mein table-name access hamesha DCL bypass karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: SE16N in SAP GUI accesses database tables directly — it constructs a direct SELECT on the physical table, completely bypassing the CDS layer and any associated DCL. DCL only activates when a CDS view is accessed — not when the underlying table is queried directly. To protect sensitive data from SE16N bypass: restrict SE16N access via authorization object S_TABU_DIS and table groups for sensitive tables. Alternatively, educate users to access data through CDS-view-based Fiori apps rather than SE16N. In newer SAP systems, you can also access a CDS view name directly in SE16N — in that case the DCL is applied. But table-name access in SE16N always bypasses DCL.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Security gap found in audit: Salary data CDS view had DCL restricting to HR users only. But payroll table PA0008 was accessible to developers via SE16N — they could see all salary data directly. Fix implemented: Table authorization group for PA0008 restricted to HR team only via S_TABU_DIS. Now even SE16N shows authorization error for non-HR users on PA0008. DCL protects CDS access; table auth group protects direct table access. Defense in depth.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: Sirf DCL lagakar sochna ki data completely secure hai — SE16N aur direct table access routes still open hain. Security ka multi-layer approach chahiye: DCL for CDS consumers, table authorization groups for direct access, profile restrictions for SE16N.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" DCL protects CDS view access
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE
  as select from pa0008
{ key pa0008.pernr, pa0008.lga01, pa0008.bet01 }

" DCL: ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE
@MappingRole: true
define role ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE {
  grant select on ZCDS_SALARY_SECURE
    where (pernr)
           = aspect pfcg_auth( P_PERNR,
                               PSIGN, ACTVT = '03' );
}

" SE16N STILL bypasses above DCL if user types:
" Table Name: PA0008 → direct access, no DCL!

" ABAP: protect direct table access separately
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_TABU_DIS'
  ID 'DICBERCLS' FIELD 'PA'
  ID 'ACTVT'    FIELD '03'.
IF sy-subrc <> 0.
  MESSAGE 'No authorization for table PA0008' TYPE 'E'.
ENDIF.

followupAnswerBank.question: What is inherited access control in CDS views?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: Jab ek CDS view dusri CDS view ke upar build hoti hai, ye apni base view se access control inherit kar sakti hai @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT use karke. Is case mein, consuming view ko apni khud ki DCL nahi chahiye — woh row-level security inherit karti hai us view se jisse woh select karti hai. Ye layered CDS architectures mein useful hai: DCL ek baar interface view pe define karo, aur saari consumption views jo uske upar built hain automatically same access control inherit karti hain. Ye duplication kam karta hai aur security maintenance centralize karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: When a CDS view is built on top of another CDS view, it can inherit the access control from its base view using @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT. In this case, the consuming view does not need its own DCL — it inherits the row-level security from the view it selects from. This is useful in layered CDS architectures: define DCL once on the interface view, and all consumption views built on it automatically inherit the same access control. This reduces duplication and centralizes security maintenance.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Layered CDS architecture security: ZI_SALES_ORDER (interface view) had DCL restricting by VKORG. Four consumption views built on ZI_SALES_ORDER: ZC_SO_LIST, ZC_SO_DETAIL, ZC_SO_ANALYTICS, ZC_SO_FIORI. Each set @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT. No separate DCL needed for any consumption view. When VKORG authorization rules changed, only the interface view DCL was updated — all four consumption views automatically applied the new rules. Four views, one DCL maintenance point.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT use karna jab base view mein #NOT_REQUIRED set hai — child view bhi unprotected ho jaata hai unintentionally. Hamesha check karo ki base view ka authorization check properly set hai before using inheritance in child views.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Interface view with own DCL
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
define view ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE
  as select from vbak
{ key vbak.vbeln, vbak.vkorg, vbak.kunnr }

" DCL for interface view (DCL object: ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE)
" ... pfcg_auth on VKORG ...

" Consumption views - inherit security from base
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck:
  #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT
define view ZC_SALES_ORDER_LIST
  as select from ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE
{ vbeln, vkorg, kunnr }  " inherits DCL from base

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck:
  #INHERITED_FROM_DELEGATED_CONTEXT
define view ZC_SALES_ORDER_ANALYTICS
  as select from ZI_SALES_ORDER_BASE
{ vkorg, count(*) as OrderCount }  " inherits DCL

followupAnswerBank.question: How do you test if DCL is working correctly?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DCL testing mein verify karna involve hota hai ki users sirf apna authorized data subset hi dekhte hain. Limited authorizations wala test user create karo ya use karo — for example, VKORG authorization mein sirf ek sales organization. Us user ki tarah login karo ya SU53 transaction use karke authorization failures check karo. CDS view se SELECT run karo — result mein sirf user ki authorizations se matching data hona chahiye. Privileged user ke result se compare karke filtering verify karo. ST01 Authorization Trace use karo ye dekhne ke liye ki runtime pe kaunse authorization objects check ho rahe hain. Eclipse ADT mein, CDS data preview (F8) logged-in developer ki authorizations ke under run karta hai.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: Testing DCL involves verifying that users see only their authorized data subset. Create or use a test user with limited authorizations — for example, only one sales organization in VKORG authorization. Log in as that user or use transaction SU53 to check authorization failures. Run a SELECT FROM the CDS view — the result should contain only data matching the user's authorizations. Compare with a privileged user's result to verify filtering. Use ST01 Authorization Trace to see which authorization objects are being checked at runtime. In Eclipse ADT, the CDS data preview (F8) runs under the logged-in developer's authorizations.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: DCL test scenario in project: DCL written for airline carrier authorization. Testing: created test user ZTESTUSER01 with S_CARRID for only 'LH' (Lufthansa). Logged in as ZTESTUSER01. SELECT FROM ZCDS_AIRLINE_SECURE returned only LH records. ST01 trace confirmed S_CARRID check triggered. Then tested with ZTESTUSER02 having 'AA' and 'LH' — two airlines returned. Admin user (all CARRID auth) — all airlines returned. DCL confirmed working correctly across three user scenarios.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: DCL test karna own admin/developer user se — jo likely has full authorizations. DCL filter nazar nahi aata kyunki sab authorized hai. Always test with a restricted user. Aur: ST01 trace use karna — shows exactly which auth checks are happening.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" Testing DCL - ABAP test program
REPORT z_test_dcl_check.

" Run this as a restricted test user
" (not as admin with full authorizations)

" Step 1: Direct SELECT from CDS view
SELECT AirlineID, AirlineName
  FROM zcds_airline_secure
  INTO TABLE @DATA(lt_authorized).

WRITE: / 'Records visible to current user:',
         lines( lt_authorized ).
LOOP AT lt_authorized INTO DATA(ls_airline).
  WRITE: / ls_airline-AirlineID, ls_airline-AirlineName.
ENDLOOP.

" Step 2: Cross-check - what authorization user has?
AUTHORITY-CHECK OBJECT 'S_CARRID'
  ID 'CARRID' FIELD 'LH'
  ID 'ACTVT'  FIELD '03'.
WRITE: / 'LH authorized?', sy-subrc.  " 0 = yes

followupAnswerBank.question: What is the pfcg_auth aspect in DCL and how does it map to PFCG roles?
followupAnswerBank.hinglishExplanation: DCL mein pfcg_auth aspect ek built-in function hai jo user ki PFCG-maintained role assignments se runtime pe authorization values read karta hai. Ye ek specific authorization object reference karta hai, uske authorization fields ko CDS view fields se map karta hai, aur optionally fixed activity code specify karta hai jaise display ke liye ACTVT = '03'. Query time pe, system specified authorization object field mein user ki saari authorized values retrieve karta hai, aur unhe corresponding CDS view field pe IN filter ki tarah apply karta hai. Iska matlab authorization maintenance PFCG roles mein rehti hai — business admins maintain karte hain kaun kya dekhega bina DCL code touch kiye.
followupAnswerBank.interviewAnswer: The pfcg_auth aspect in DCL is a built-in function that reads authorization values from the user's PFCG-maintained role assignments at runtime. It references a specific authorization object, maps its authorization fields to CDS view fields, and optionally specifies a fixed activity code like ACTVT = '03' for display. At query time, the system retrieves all values the user is authorized for in the specified authorization object field, and applies them as an IN filter on the corresponding CDS view field. This means authorization maintenance stays in PFCG roles — business admins maintain who sees what without touching DCL code.
followupAnswerBank.realtimeExplanation: Role-based access in practice: Sales order CDS view with DCL using pfcg_auth on V_VBAK_VKO for VKORG. Role ZSALES_GERMANY: VKORG = '1000'. Role ZSALES_USA: VKORG = '2000'. Role ZSALES_ALL: VKORG = '*' (all). Assign roles to users in SU01/PFCG. User with ZSALES_GERMANY sees only VKORG 1000 orders. No DCL change needed to add a new VKORG — just update the PFCG role. Clean separation: DCL defines the rule, PFCG maintains the values.
followupAnswerBank.mistakes: Galti: PFCG role mein authorization values maintain nahi karna — DCL pfcg_auth se koi value nahi milti, user kuch bhi nahi dekh pata. PFCG role maintenance aur DCL setup dono parallel mein karne chahiye. Test: user ke role mein sahi authorization values hain ki nahi — SU53 se verify karo.
followupAnswerBank.codeExample:
" PFCG authorization setup (maintained in SU01/PFCG):
" Authorization Object: V_VBAK_VKO
"   Field VKORG: 1000 (sales org Germany)
"   Field ACTVT: 03 (display)

" DCL maps pfcg_auth to CDS view field
@MappingRole: true
define role ZCDS_SALES_ORDER_DCL {
  grant select on ZCDS_SALES_ORDER_SECURE
    where (SalesOrg, DistChannel)
